scenario.name: missing_prd_security_section
scenario.trigger: plan/prd.md exists but has no Security & Privacy NFRs (section 9) or the section is empty.

required_actions.action: Do not proceed with security planning without security requirements.
Report to the user: PRD section 9 (Security & Privacy NFRs) is required.
Recommend updating the PRD to add security requirements, or ask the user to provide security constraints.
If user insists on proceeding: derive minimal security requirements from data types and integrations in the PRD, and document assumptions.

prohibited_actions.action: Do not invent security requirements without user acknowledgment.

scenario.name: unclear_data_handling
scenario.trigger: PRD describes data but handling (storage, retention, sharing) is unclear or contradictory.

required_actions.action: Surface the ambiguity to the user.
Ask: classification, retention, sharing boundaries, deletion requirements.
Document assumptions if user cannot clarify, and flag for follow-up.

scenario.name: unresolvable_compliance_question
scenario.trigger: Compliance requirement is unclear (e.g., "might need HIPAA" or "could be GDPR") and user cannot determine applicability.

required_actions.action: Document the uncertainty and the compliance options.
Recommend consulting legal or compliance experts for definitive answer.
Include a placeholder in the security plan: "Compliance: [GDPR/HIPAA/etc.] — TBD. Recommend legal review."
Design controls that would satisfy the strictest likely requirement, with note that scope may be reduced after clarification.

scenario.name: conflicting_security_and_usability
scenario.trigger: User requests a design that conflicts with security (e.g., no auth for sensitive data, plaintext credentials, weak session handling).

required_actions.action: Surface the conflict explicitly: "This design conflicts with [security requirement] because [reason]."
Present alternatives that satisfy both security and usability.
If user insists on the insecure option: require explicit risk acknowledgment and document as accepted risk.
Never silently accept an insecure design.

prohibited_actions.action: Do not accept insecure design without explicit user acknowledgment.
